items.title: Lemon Garlic Chicken Recipe with Yogurt (Mediterranean Style)

expectedSnapshot.title: Lemon Garlic Chicken Recipe with Yogurt (Mediterranean Style)

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Marinate: Mix marinade ingredients, coat chicken, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Make Sauce: Whisk sauce ingredients until smooth. Set aside.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Sear: Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high. Cook chicken 5–6 mins per side until golden and cooked through.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Plate: Spread sauce on a plate, top with chicken, and garnish with parsley and lemon.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: The Yogurt Secret: Using full-fat Greek yogurt is essential; it creates a protective barrier that keeps the chicken juicy under high heat.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Pro Prep: If you have the time, marinate the chicken for a full 24 hours. The lactic acid in the yogurt works slowly to break down fibers, resulting in a texture far superior to quick-marinated poultry.Olive Oil

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Serving Suggestion: This dish is traditionally served with warm pita or over a bed of fluffy bulgur to soak up the extra garlic sauce.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Lemon Garlic Chicken Ingredients Overview

method.title: Step 9
method.body: 4.1 Chicken Marinade Ingredients

method.title: Step 10
method.body: To create the best lemon garlic chicken marinade, you’ll need:

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Chicken Breast or Tenderloins: These lean cuts soak up the marinade beautifully.

method.title: Step 12
method.body: Fresh Lemon Juice: Acts as a secondary tenderizer.

method.title: Step 13
method.body: Garlic: Crushed or finely minced to permeate the meat.

method.title: Step 14
method.body: Olive Oil: Helps with the “Maillard reaction” (that beautiful golden browning).

method.title: Step 15
method.body: 4.2 Lemon Garlic Yogurt Sauce Ingredients

method.title: Step 16
method.body: This sauce is so good you’ll want to put it on a Mezze Platter Recipe.

method.title: Step 17
method.body: Greek Yogurt: The base of our sauce.

method.title: Step 18
method.body: Tahini: This is the “secret ingredient” that makes the sauce feel rich and professional.

method.title: Step 19
method.body: Extra Virgin Olive Oil: For silkiness.

method.title: Step 20
method.body: Water: Just a splash to reach your desired consistency.

method.title: Step 21
method.body: 4.3 Garnish Ingredients

method.title: Step 22
method.body: Don’t skip the finish! Fresh parsley adds an earthy bitterness that balances the lemon, while extra lemon wedges allow guests to customize their acidity level.Fruits & Vegetables

method.title: Step 23
method.body: Step-by-Step Instructions For Lemon Garlic Chicken Recipe

method.title: Step 24
method.body: 5.1 Marinating the Chicken

method.title: Step 25
method.body: Raw chicken coating in yogurt garlic marinade.

method.title: Step 26
method.body: The yogurt marinade tenderizes the chicken perfectly.

method.title: Step 27
method.body: In a large bowl, whisk together your yogurt, lemon juice, minced garlic, and olive oil. Pat your chicken dry (this is important!) and toss it in the mixture until every inch is coated.Greek Yogurt

method.title: Step 28
method.body: Pro Tip: While 30 minutes will give you flavor, 12–24 hours in the fridge will give you a texture that melts in your mouth. The yogurt enzymes work slowly to tenderize the meat without making it “mushy” like vinegar-based marinades can.

method.title: Step 29
method.body: 5.2 Preparing the Lemon Garlic Yogurt Sauce

method.title: Step 30
method.body: Creamy lemon garlic yogurt sauce with tahini.

method.title: Step 31
method.body: This tahini-yogurt sauce is the perfect pairing.

method.title: Step 32
method.body: While the chicken marinates, whisk your sauce ingredients in a small bowl. Start with the yogurt and tahini, then slowly add lemon juice and water until it’s the consistency of a thick pourable dressing. Let it rest in the fridge for at least 20 minutes—this allows the raw garlic “bite” to mellow out and harmonize with the tahini.Juice

method.title: Step 33
method.body: 5.3 Cooking the Chicken Perfectly

method.title: Step 34
method.body: Pan-searing yogurt marinated chicken in a skillet.

method.title: Step 35
method.body: Achieving that perfect golden crust.

method.title: Step 36
method.body: Use a heavy-bottomed skillet or cast iron pan. Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat. Do not overcrowd the pan. If you put too many pieces in at once, the temperature drops and the chicken will steam in its own juices rather than sear.Chicken

method.title: Step 37
method.body: Cook for about 5–6 minutes per side. You are looking for deep golden-brown charred spots. The internal temperature should reach 165°F (74°C).

method.title: Step 38
method.body: 5.4 Warming the Sauce Safely

method.title: Step 39
method.body: If you prefer a warm sauce, do not put it in a pot over high heat—yogurt will curdle and separate. Instead, turn off the heat in your skillet after removing the chicken, and let the residual heat of the pan warm the sauce slightly, or simply serve it at room temperature against the hot chicken for a refreshing contrast.

method.title: Step 40
method.body: Close up of a bite of lemon garlic chicken.

method.title: Step 41
method.body: Tender, juicy, and full of flavor.

method.title: Step 42
method.body: To match our signature look:Yogurt

method.title: Step 43
method.body: Spoon a generous pool of the yogurt sauce onto a rustic neutral plate.

method.title: Step 44
method.body: Arrange the golden chicken pieces on top.

method.title: Step 45
method.body: Drizzle with high-quality olive oil.

method.title: Step 46
method.body: Shower with chopped parsley and place two lemon wedges on the side.

method.title: Step 47
method.body: Pro Tips for the Best Lemon Garlic Chicken

method.title: Step 48
method.body: Fresh is King: Never use bottled lemon juice. The flavor is dull and metallic compared to the bright zing of a fresh lemon.

method.title: Step 49
method.body: Room Temp: Take the marinated chicken out of the fridge 15 minutes before cooking so it cooks evenly.

method.title: Step 50
method.body: The Rest: Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before slicing. This keeps the juices inside the meat.

method.title: Step 51
method.body: Chicken Breast vs. Chicken Thighs?

method.title: Step 52
method.body: You can absolutely use lemon garlic chicken thighs! Thighs are more forgiving and stay juicy longer. If using boneless, skinless thighs, increase the cook time by about 2–3 minutes per side. If you prefer a leaner, quicker meal, stick with the lemon garlic chicken breast.

method.title: Step 53
method.body: How Long Should You Marinate?

method.title: Step 54
method.body: Minimum: 30 minutes (surface flavor only).

method.title: Step 55
method.body: Ideal: 6 to 12 hours.

method.title: Step 56
method.body: Maximum: 24 hours. Beyond this, the acid in the lemon can start to “cook” the chicken and affect the texture negatively.

method.title: Step 57
method.body: Is This Recipe Healthy?

method.title: Step 58
method.body: Yes! This is a hallmark of the Mediterranean Diet. It is packed with lean protein, heart-healthy monounsaturated fats from olive oil and tahini, and probiotics from the yogurt. It is a much lighter alternative to traditional “creamy” garlic chicken recipes that use heavy cream or butter.Flora & Fauna

method.title: Step 59
method.body: What to Serve with Lemon Garlic Yogurt Chicken Recipe

method.title: Step 60
method.body: Mediterranean dinner spread with yogurt chicken and sides.

method.title: Step 61
method.body: A complete, healthy Mediterranean meal.

method.title: Step 62
method.body: Grains: Serve over a Greek Quinoa Salad Recipe or fluffy basmati rice.

method.title: Step 63
method.body: Vegetables: A side of roasted broccoli recipe or a crisp Fattoush recipe.

method.title: Step 64
method.body: The Full Spread: Add some hummus recipe and warm pita bread for a true feast.

method.title: Step 65
method.body: Lemon Garlic Chicken Storage & Reheating

method.title: Step 66
method.body: Store the chicken and sauce in separate airtight containers in the fridge for up to 3 days. When reheating, do it gently in a pan over low heat. Avoid the microwave if possible, as it can make the chicken rubbery.

method.title: Step 67
method.body: Lemon Garlic Chicken Recipe Variations

method.title: Step 68
method.body: Spicy: Add a teaspoon of Aleppo pepper or red chili flakes to the marinade.

method.title: Step 69
method.body: Bowl Style: Serve over a bed of spinach with cucumbers, tomatoes, and pickled onions.

method.title: Step 70
method.body: Dairy-Free: Use an unsweetened almond or cashew-based yogurt.

expectedSnapshot.title: Lighter Pickle Lemonade Chicken Tacos
ingredients: 1 tsp avocado oil or olive oil, or cooking spray
Salt and black pepper, to taste
Optional: guacamole, avocado, Greek yogurt, lime wedges

method.title: Step 1
method.body: In a bowl, mix the grated garlic, lime juice, hot sauce, onion powder, 1 tsp oil, salt, and black pepper.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Add the chicken thighs and coat them well in the marinade. Marinate for at least 15 minutes, or longer if possible.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Heat a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Use cooking spray if needed.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Sear the chicken thighs until browned and fully cooked, about 6–8 minutes per side, depending on thickness.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Remove the chicken to a cutting board and chop into bite-sized pieces.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Add 1/2 cup water to the skillet and scrape up the browned bits to create a pan sauce.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Return the chopped chicken to the pan and simmer for about 3 minutes, until coated in the sauce.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Serve each portion of chicken with 3 warm corn tortillas, minced onion, cilantro, salsa, and optional toppings.

method.title: Step 8

expectedSnapshot.title: maple soy glazed chicken thighs 🤤
ingredients: 450g Thinly Sliced Beef (Flat Iron as its leaner)

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Into a bowl add the following ingredients: 2 Tbsp Gochujang, 1 Tbsp Gochugaru, 2 Tbsp Minced Garlic , 2 Tbsp Soy Sauce, 1⁄2 Tbsp Sesame Oil, 5g Honey, 2 Tbsp Splenda, 2 Tsp Black pepper, 2 Tsp Salt and mix.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Into a large bowl, combine the beef, onions, carrots and marinade. Mix well and let the mixture marinade for a minimum of 30min and up to 24hours (Longer the better).

method.title: Step 3
method.body: On a medium high heat, spray your pan with cooking spray and place the meat. Spread out evenly.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Stir occasionally, separating any visible clumps. Take off the heat when the beef is finished cooking and the liquid has turned into a crystallized glaze (thickened).

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Separate into 2 equal portions, serve on 125g of white rice, garnish with sesame seeds, green onions and enjoy!

expectedSnapshot.title: Marry Me Chicken
expectedSnapshot.author: Naz Deravian
ingredients: 3large boneless, skinless chicken breasts, or 6 chicken cutlets (about 21/4 pounds total), patted dry
Kosher salt (such as Diamond Crystal) and black pepper
3tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, plus more as needed
1/3cup sliced sun-dried tomatoes, packed in oil
Leche evaporada o nata ligera: 120 ml
Caldo, ajo, tomate concentrado y especias igual

method.title: Step 1
method.body: If using chicken breasts, start from the thickest end and slice each chicken breast in half horizontally so you end up with a total of 6 cutlets (see Tip). Season both sides of the chicken cutlets well with salt and pepper.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Scatter the flour on a large plate and coat the cutlets, shaking off the excess. Transfer the cutlets to a sheet pan or large plate in a single layer.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium-high. Once hot, reduce the heat to medium and add the butter. As soon as it melts, add the cutlets and cook until golden on one side, about 5 minutes. Flip the chicken and cook the other side until golden, 4 to 5 minutes. Do this in batches, if needed, adding more oil, if needed. Transfer the cutlets to a plate or sheet pan.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Reduce the heat to low, add the garlic and cook, stirring often, until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. Add the tomato paste, stirring until the color deepens, about 2 minutes. Add the oregano and red-pepper flakes, to taste.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Increase the heat to medium, add the stock and bring to a simmer, scraping up any bits from the bottom of the pan, until the liquid is reduced by half, about 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Step 6

method.title: Step 9
method.body: Add 1⁄2 cup of the cream and warm through, stirring, until it thickens slightly, about 3 minutes. Watch the cream closely, reducing the heat if necessary, to maintain a gentle simmer. Stir in the Parmesan and the sun-dried tomatoes. Add more cream, if you like, and season the sauce.

method.title: Step 10
method.body: Step 7

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Place the chicken back in the pan to warm through, about 4 minutes. Remove from the heat and scatter basil on top.

selected.title: Marry Me Chicken
selected.author: Naz Deravian
ingredients: 3 large boneless, skinless chicken breasts, or 6 chicken cutlets (about 2¼ lb total)
3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil, plus more as needed
⅓ cup sliced sun-dried tomatoes in oil

method.title: Step 1
method.body: If using chicken breasts, slice each horizontally into two cutlets and season well.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Coat the chicken in flour and shake off the excess.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Heat the oil, add the butter and cook the cutlets until golden on both sides. Work in batches if needed, then transfer to a plate.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Reduce the heat, add the garlic and cook for 1–2 minutes. Add the tomato paste, oregano and red-pepper flakes and cook for another 2 minutes.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Add the stock and simmer until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Add ½ cup cream and simmer gently until slightly thickened. Stir in the parmesan and sun-dried tomatoes, adding more cream if desired.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Return the chicken to the pan for about 4 minutes, then finish with basil.

items.title: Meal Prep Roasted Chicken and Red Pepper Pasta Salad

expectedSnapshot.title: Meal Prep Roasted Chicken and Red Pepper Pasta Salad
ingredients: 1/2 tsp sea salt (3g) – adjust to 3/4 tsp (4g) if needed
1/4 tsp red pepper flakes (1g) – increase to 1/2 tsp (2g) for more heat

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Roast the red bell peppers. Spray with olive oil or avocado oil spray and air fry at 400°F for 10 minutes. Remove the stem and seeds, then add straight to the blender.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Blend all roasted red pepper sauce ingredients until smooth.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Cook pasta 1-2 minutes under al dente and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process. This keeps the texture firm even after refrigeration.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Toss romaine, pasta, chicken, bell peppers, tomatoes, and sauce together.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: 👌🏻 Split into 4 meal prep containers. The romaine stays crisp, but you can store the sauce separately for extra freshness. Also delicious served cold!

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Cut the 2 lbs chicken thighs into 11⁄2 to 2 inch pieces and add them to a bowl. Toss with 1 tsp olive oil, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp onion powder, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp Aleppo pepper, 1⁄4 tsp cinnamon, 3 pinches salt, 3 pinches black pepper, 11⁄2 tbsp tomato paste, 2 tbsp plain yogurt, and the juice of 1 lemon. Mix well until the chicken is evenly coated. Marinate for 2-6 hours, or ideally overnight.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers (adding onions or peppers if desired). Place on a sheet pan and cook in the oven at 400°F for 32-35 minutes, until cooked through and lightly charred.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: (Alternatively, cook in the air fryer at 400°F for about 25 minutes, or grill over medium-high heat until the chicken is fully cooked.)

method.title: Step 4
method.body: In a small bowl, mix 3 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp mayonnaise, 3 tbsp sour cream, juice of 1⁄2 lemon, lemon zest, 2 tbsp chopped parsley, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Stir well and thin with a little water if needed until the sauce reaches your desired consistency.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: In a bowl, combine 1⁄2 pint halved cherry tomatoes, 1⁄3 diced red onion, 3 tbsp chopped parsley, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp sumac, and a pinch of salt. Toss gently and adjust seasoning to taste.

selected.title: MEDITERRANEAN CHICKEN SKEWERS

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Cut the chicken into 4–5 cm pieces and coat with the oil, spices, tomato paste, yogurt and lemon juice. Marinate for 2–6 hours or overnight.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Thread the chicken onto skewers and bake at 200°C for 32–35 minutes, until cooked and lightly charred. Alternatively, air-fry at 200°C for about 25 minutes or grill.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Mix the yogurt-sauce ingredients, adding water as needed.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Combine the tomato-salad ingredients.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Divide into five servings and serve with the tomato salad, yogurt sauce and rice or quinoa if desired.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: In a smaller bowl, mix together 4 tablespoons olive oil, 1 tbsp tomato paste, the zest of half a lemon, the juice of 1/2 large lemon, 1 tbsp fresh oregano, dill, or parsley, 2 thinly sliced garlic cloves, and a generous pinch of salt. Mix well. Then add to the shrimp and mix well, ensuring the shrimp is evenly coated with the mixture. .

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Bring a non toxic non stick pan to medium heat, add the shrimp and cook for 60-90 seconds on first side, flip, and another 60-ish seconds on second side. You dont want to overcook shrimp as it becomes rubbery. .

method.title: Step 3
method.body: For the yogurt sauce, mix together 1.5 cup plain yogurt, 1 finely diced Persian cucumber, 1 handful of fresh dill (about 3 tablespoons), 1 green chili or jalapeño pepper with most of the seeds removed, a couple squeezes of lemon juice, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix well, then taste and adjust to your liking. .

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Plate things up, and enjoy! I spread some of the yogurt sauce as the base, topped with shrimp, and topped it all with a dusting of Aleppo pepper and fresh dill - any fresh herb will do. . Again, you can enjoy this with rice, quinoa, or if you’re lowcarb, my high protein Kaizen rice. . ENJOY. . . . .

method.title: Step 1

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Rinse the sushi rice under cold water a few times until it runs clear, then drain. Add to a small saucepan with the spinach water and bring to the boil. Once boiling, cover with a tight-fitting lid, turn the heat down to low and cook for 12 mins. Turn off the heat and leave to steam (lid on) for another 12 mins.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: In a mixing bowl, whisk together the miso, honey, soy sauce, lemon juice, MSG (if using), a pinch of salt and some black pepper. Slice each chicken thigh into three strips, then toss through the miso marinade.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: On a tray, lay out the chicken and spoon over any extra sauce. Grill for 6–8 mins until charred and cooked through.

method.title: Step 5

expectedSnapshot.title: Old-Fashioned Beef Stew
expectedSnapshot.author: Molly O'Neill
ingredients: 1pound beef stewing meat, trimmed and cut into inch cubes
31/2cups beef broth, homemade or low-sodium canned
5medium carrots, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch rounds
2large baking potatoes, peeled and cut into 3/4-inch cubes

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Combine the flour and pepper in a bowl, add the beef and toss to coat well. Heat 3 teaspoons of the oil in a large pot. Add the beef a few pieces at a time; do not overcrowd. Cook, turning the pieces until beef is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per batch; add more oil as needed between batches.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Remove the beef from the pot and add the vinegar and wine. Cook over medium-high heat, scraping the pan with a wooden spoon to loosen any browned bits. Add the beef, beef broth and bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a slow simmer.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Cover and cook, skimming broth from time to time, until the beef is tender, about 11⁄2 hours. Add the onions and carrots and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes. Add the potatoes and simmer until vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes more. Add broth or water if the stew is dry. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Ladle among 4 bowls and serve.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: FAQS

method.title: Step 7
method.body: How to thicken beef stew

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Starches, like the flour, cornstarch and potatoes, thicken beef stew. Sometimes, beef cubes are coated in flour, then browned, leaving flour in the pan while sealing flour to the meat. When the meat is later simmered, that flour thickens the liquid as do potatoes, which release their starches as they cook. Cornstarch is often stirred with cold water into a slurry that's then simmered in the cooking liquid to thicken it.

Add the potatoes and simmer until vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes more. Add broth or water if the stew is dry. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Ladle among 4 bowls and serve.

method.title: Step 4

When the meat is later simmered, that flour thickens the liquid as do potatoes, which release their starches as they cook. Cornstarch is often stirred with cold water into a slurry that's then simmered in the cooking liquid to thicken it.

items.title: One Pan Chicken with Roasted Sweet Potato & Broccoli

expectedSnapshot.title: One Pan Chicken with Roasted Sweet Potato & Broccoli
expectedSnapshot.author: Arash Hashemi
ingredients: 4 chicken leg quarters (or 2 lb chicken thighs)
Zest and juice of 1 large lemon
Generous pinch of salt and fresh cracked pepper

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Add 4 chicken leg quarters (or 2 lb thighs) to a large bowl. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, then add the zest and juice of a large lemon, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp oregano, 1/2 tsp cayenne, 1.5 tbsp paprika, and a generous pinch of salt and pepper. Add 2 tbsp tomato paste and 4 minced garlic cloves. Mix really well so every piece is coated.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Let it marinate for at least 2 hours, or overnight if you have the time. The longer it sits, the deeper the flavor goes.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: When you’re ready to cook, preheat your oven to 400°F.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Dice 2 medium sweet potatoes and slice 1 large shallot (or 1/2 red onion). Grab a large handful of broccoli florets. The key here is to dice everything pretty small so it cooks at the same pace as the chicken. If you cut the veggies too big, they won’t be done in time.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Place the veggies at the bottom of an oven-safe dish or sheet pan. This part is important. The veggies go under the chicken so they soak up all the juices while it bakes. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, 1/2 tbsp oregano, and 1/2 tbsp garlic powder. Toss to coat.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Lay the marinated chicken on top of the veggies. Pop the whole thing in the oven at 400°F for 40 minutes.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Now the move that takes this from good to so good: turn the oven off and let the pan sit inside for another 20–30 minutes. The residual heat keeps cooking the veggies gently and they soak in even more of the juices. Don’t skip this step.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: While that’s resting, make the sauce. Mix 3 tbsp mayo with 1 tbsp tomato paste, 1 minced garlic clove, the zest and juice of 1 lemon, and a pinch of salt. Stir until smooth. Taste it and adjust the lemon or salt if it needs it.

expectedSnapshot.title: ONE PAN GARLIC CHICKEN PRIMAVERA
ingredients: 3/4 to 1 lb. boneless chicken breasts, sliced thin (1/2” thick) • 2 tsp. sea salt, divided • 1/2 lb. zucchini, sliced • 1/2 lb. asparagus, chopped • 3/4 cup cherry tomatoes • 2-3 garlic cloves,minced • 1 tsp. fresh cracked pepper • 1 tsp. dried oregano • 1/2 cup frozen peas • 1 cup cream • 3/4 cup chicken or vegetable broth • 2 large handfuls spinach •

method.title: Step 1
method.body: heat 1 tbsp. olive or avocado oil in a large cast iron or nonstick pan; season chicken breasts with 1 tsp. salt and sear until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes per side. remove from pan.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: add zucchini and asparagus to the same pan; saute 2-3 minutes. add cherry tomatoes, garlic, remaining salt, pepper, oregano, and peas. stir to combine. pour in cream and broth; bring to a light boil and simmer for 5 minutes to thicken the sauce. stir in spinach and cook until wilted/

method.title: Step 3
method.body: add chicken back to the pan and top with grated parmesan or pecorino romano cheese, if desired.

selected.title: ONE PAN GARLIC CHICKEN PRIMAVERA
ingredients: ¾–1 lb boneless chicken breasts, sliced about 1.25 cm thick
¾ cup chicken or vegetable stock
Grated Parmesan or Pecorino Romano, optional

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Heat the oil in a large pan. Season the chicken with half the salt and sear for about 5 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through. Remove from the pan.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Add the zucchini and asparagus and sauté for 2–3 minutes. Add the tomatoes, garlic, remaining salt, pepper, oregano and peas.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Pour in the cream and stock. Bring to a gentle boil and simmer for about 5 minutes, until slightly thickened. Stir in the spinach until wilted.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Return the chicken to the pan and finish with grated cheese, if using.

expectedSnapshot.title: One-Pot High-Protein Chicken Biryani (Oven Method)
expectedSnapshot.author: Deadpan Diya
ingredients: 1 1⁄3 lb (600 g) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, diced
1 cup (200 g) basmati rice, well washed
2 Tbsp + 1 tsp (35 ml) ghee or vegetable oil
2 bay leaves, 2 cinnamon sticks, 4 cardamom pods, 4 cloves
2 red onions, thinly sliced; 2 tomatoes, chopped
1 tsp (5 g) ginger paste; 1 tsp (5 g) garlic paste
1 1⁄2 tsp (4 g) coriander, 1 tsp (3 g) turmeric, 1 tsp (2 g) cayenne, 1 tsp (3 g) garam masala
1⁄2 tsp (1 g) cumin seeds (or ground), 1⁄2 tsp (1 g) black pepper, salt to taste
3 Tbsp (45 ml) coconut milk, 1 1⁄4 cups (270 ml) chicken broth
To serve: cilantro, mint, lime, Greek yogurt

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Soak rice: Rinse well, then soak in cold water for min. 20 mins, preheat oven 320°F (160°C)

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Bloom spices: Heat 1 tsp oil; fry bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves 5 sec; set aside

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Chicken masala – 10 min: Heat 2 Tbsp oil; fry onions 3 min. Add ginger & garlic 1 min, tomatoes 5 min. Mix spices with 1–2 Tbsp water; toss with chicken 1–2 min (don’t cook through).

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Assemble: Add drained rice to dish. Pour coconut milk + broth; stir. Tuck in whole spices

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Bake – Cover; bake 320°F (160°C) 35-40 min (adjust based on cookware note). Check at 35 mins, if rice isn’t cooked add 2-4 tbsps of water or broth, cover and cook another 5-10mins

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Rest – 10 min: Let sit 10 min; fluff with fork

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Serve: Garnish with cilantro, mint, lime; serve with yogurt

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Preheat oven to 400F.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Add 4 bone-in skin-on chicken thighs to bowl. You can use 2lbs of thighs, drumsticks, or any chicken piece you like. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil. Season with salt & pepper, the zest + juice of a large lemon, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp oregano, 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper, & 1 tbsp sweet paprika. To add flavor and color, add 2 tbsp tomato paste and 3-4 minced garlic cloves. Mix really well, and if you have the time, let this marinate for a couple hours.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Next, I cut up half a butternut squash into small cubes (this is key so it cooks all the way through!) and sliced up a handful broccoli florets.Drizzle with olive oil, season with a pinch of salt, 1/2 tbsp oregano, and 1/2 tbsp garlic powder, and place at the bottom of an oven safe pan.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Place the chicken on top of the veggies, and if there is any leftover chicken marinate spoon it over the veggies. Pop into the oven at 400F for 40 mins. Once done, turn off the heat then let the pan sit in the oven for 30 minutes without the heat on to soften up the veggies and soak up the flavors even more.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: While that’s going, make your sauce by mixing 3 tbsp mayo, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, the zest and juice of 1 small lemon, and a pinch of salt. You can add a tablespoon or two of water to thin it out, as needed.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Remove pan from the oven, plate it up, top with finely chopped fresh herbs, a little sauce, and enjoy.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: If you make this, and you want more healthy recipes, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

expectedSnapshot.title: Paprika Chicken and Rice
ingredients: Chicken thighs: I always buy them bone-in and skin-on. The bone keeps the meat tender during cooking and the skin renders down beautifully in the pan, adding flavour and texture to the whole dish. You can use boneless, skinless thighs but you will lose some of that juiciness and depth. Chicken marylands or a combination of thighs and drumsticks also work great here.
Chicken stock: You could swap this for vegetable stock if needed, but chicken stock really is the best match here as it deepens the flavour of both the chicken and the rice.
4 chicken thighs, bone in, skin on

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Chicken prep and first cook

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Combine the tomato paste, half of the olive oil, paprika, chilli powder and lemon juice in a large bowl.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Mix well, then add the chicken and toss to coat the chicken completely.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Place the remaining oil in a large heavy-based frying pan and add the chicken, skin side down.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Put over a medium heat and cook for 6-8 minutes, until the chicken skin renders and starts to brown. Transfer to a tray. It won’t be completely cooked at this stage but that’s okay as we’ll add it back to the pan soon.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Cook the base

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Add the rice to a bowl of water, enough so it’s completely covered.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Dice the red onion and finely dice the carrot. Add to the pan you cooked the chicken in, and cook for 3 minutes, stirring, until softened.

method.title: Step 9
method.body: Deglaze the pan with a little of the stock, scraping off the fond from the bottom. Add the cinnamon and cumin, season with some salt and cook for 2 minutes, until aromatic.

method.title: Step 10
method.body: Drain the rice, add it to the pan and stir through the vegetables to combine well.

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Add the remaining stock and return the chicken to the pan. Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to very low, cover with the lid and cook for 15 minutes.

method.title: Step 12
method.body: Finish and serve

method.title: Step 13
method.body: Remove the chicken and set aside on a tray. Discard the cinnamon stick.

method.title: Step 14
method.body: Give the rice a stir, then add the spinach. Cover with the lid again, turn off the heat and let sit for 2 minutes.

method.title: Step 15
method.body: Uncover and stir the spinach through the rice. Serve with the chicken on top of the rice.

method.title: Step 16
method.body: Start the chicken skin side down in a cold pan

method.title: Step 17
method.body: Place the chicken skin side down into a cold pan before turning on the heat. Starting cold gives the fat time to slowly render out, which gives you some nice colour and flavour on the chicken.

method.title: Step 18
method.body: Deglazing the pan

method.title: Step 19
method.body: After removing the chicken you’ll see caramelised bits stuck to the bottom of the pan, that’s called fond and it’s absolutely packed with flavour. Add your carrot and onions and let them cook for a couple of minutes. Then, add enough stock to loosen the fond on the bottom of the pan. That fond going into the sauce is what gives this dish its depth.

method.title: Step 20
method.body: Soak the rice first

method.title: Step 21
method.body: Soaking the rice in cold water before adding it to the pan washes off excess surface starch, which helps the grains stay fluffy and separate rather than clumping together.

method.title: Step 22
method.body: Don’t lift the lid

method.title: Step 23
method.body: Once you cover the pan and drop to very low heat, resist the urge to peek or stir. Every time you lift the lid you release the steam the rice needs to cook properly.

method.title: Step 24
method.body: Storage

method.title: Step 25
method.body: Any leftovers should be stored in an airtight container and refrigerated for up to 3 days. Reheat slowly in the oven in a casserole dish with a splash of water to loosen things up, or in the microwave until piping hot.

method.title: Step 26
method.body: FAQs

method.title: Step 27
method.body: Can I make this dish ahead of time? This dish is best made right before serving. The rice can absorb too much liquid and become mushy if it sits for too long. That said, leftovers reheat really well, so I’d recommend making a bigger batch and saving some for lunch the next day.

method.title: Step 28
method.body: Is it spicy? The chilli powder does add a mild heat to the dish. If you’re cooking for kids or just prefer no spice, reduce the amount or leave it out altogether. The smoked paprika will still give you plenty of flavour and colour without the heat.

method.title: Step 29
method.body: Can I freeze it? I wouldn’t freeze this one. The rice tends to go mushy and watery when frozen and reheated. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days instead.

method.title: Step 30
method.body: Can I make it vegetarian? This dish relies heavily on the chicken and chicken stock for its flavour, so it doesn’t translate easily into a vegetarian version. You could experiment with chickpeas and vegetable stock, but it would be quite a different dish.

method.title: Step 31
method.body: Can I make it gluten free? This recipe is naturally gluten free as written. Just double-check the label on your chicken stock to be sure, as some brands may contain gluten.

selected.title: Paprika Chicken and Rice
ingredients: ¼ tsp chilli flakes or chilli powder

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Mix the tomato paste, olive oil, smoked paprika, chilli and lemon juice. Coat the chicken and season with salt and pepper.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Place the chicken skin-side down in a cold wide pan, then set over medium heat. Cook for 6–8 minutes until the skin is golden. Turn briefly, then remove.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Rinse or soak the rice while the chicken browns.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Cook the onion and carrot in the pan until softened. Add the cinnamon and cumin, then stir in the drained rice.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Pour in the stock and return the chicken skin-side up. Cover and simmer over low heat for about 15 minutes, until the rice and chicken are cooked.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Remove the chicken and cinnamon stick. Stir the spinach into the rice, cover and rest for 2 minutes, then serve with the chicken.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Roast the veggies. Dice 1 red onion, 1 red bell pepper, 1–2 fresno peppers, 4–5 bird’s eye chilis, and 4 garlic cloves. (For less heat, just use 2 red bell peppers instead of the chilis.) Toss everything with olive oil, salt, pepper, and smoked paprika. Roast at 425°F for 45 minutes.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Blend the base. In a blender, add garlic, lemon juice + zest, olive oil, apple cider vinegar, salt, pepper, paprika, oregano, onion powder, cilantro, and a few more bird’s eye chilis if you like it extra hot.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Combine. Add in the roasted veggies and blend until super smooth.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Simmer. Pour the sauce into a saucepan, add olive oil, cover, and simmer for 15–20 minutes. Then thin with water, a little lemon juice, and season with salt.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Strain. Run the sauce through a mesh strainer into a jar. Refrigerate and you’re good to go.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Make the chicken. Toss chicken thighs with olive oil, salt, pepper, oregano, garlic, smoked paprika, and tomato paste. Roast at 425°F for ~30 minutes. At the 25-minute mark, brush with peri peri sauce and finish for 5–7 minutes.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Serve. Top with fresh cilantro, dunk in that peri peri sauce, and serve with rice, quinoa, cauli rice, or my Kaizen low-carb rice.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Absolute flavor bomb. You’ll keep this sauce on repeat.

items.title: Pillowy Mediterranean Zucchini with Garlic Feta Sauce

expectedSnapshot.title: Pillowy Mediterranean Zucchini with Garlic Feta Sauce

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Rinse 2 medium zucchini and slice them in half lengthwise.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Use a sharp knife to score the cut side in a crosshatch pattern.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Sprinkle with salt and let sit for 20-30 mins to release moisture. Dab dry with a paper towel.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Preheat your oven to 450°F.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: While the zucchini rests, make the garlic feta sauce. In a food processor, blend 3 tbsp Greek yogurt, 1 oz crumbled feta, 1 garlic clove, a handful of fresh mint, 1/4 cup lemon juice, the zest of a lemon, a couple pinches of salt and pepper.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Heat 4 tbsp avocado oil in a cast-iron or stainless pan over medium-high heat. Add zucchini cut-side down and sear for 4 to 5 minutes until golden.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Transfer the pan to the oven. Roast for 5 minutes, flip the zucchini, and roast another 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Spoon the sauce onto a plate, lay the zucchini on top (cut-side up), and finish with more crumbled feta, fresh chopped mint, crushed pistachios, lemon zest, and sumac.

expectedSnapshot.title: Pizza vietnamita
ingredients: 1 papel de arroz, los puedes comprar en herbolarios, Carrefour, Sanchez Romero, tiendas asiáticas, El Corte Inglés, etc...
1 cucharada aceite de oliva virgen extra

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Pon a calentar una sartén, añade el papel y deja a fuego medio.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Añade el huevo, rompe y extiende, añade ahora el pimentón junto con la sal y el aceite de oliva virgen extra, mezcla con el huevo, añade también el puerro cortado fino y ralla el queso.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Por último añade las espinacas, tapa la sartén y deja a fuego medio bajo durante 2/3 min más o menos.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Cierra por la mitad, ahora pulveriza aceite de oliva y dale la vuelta para que se haga por el lado del aceite, en este momento sube la sartén a fuego medio alto, roca por el otro lado y una vez que lo veas dorado dale la vuelta para que se dore por el otro lado.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: 🙏Espero que os guste tanto como a mí, gracias por todos vuestros comentarios y que tengáis un maravilloso día.

1/4 cup packed fresh mint leaves, roughly torn, plus more for serving
Raw green cabbage wedges, long beans, cucumber slices, and lettuce leaves, for serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Step 1

method.title: Step 2
method.body: In a small, dry skillet, toast the rice on medium for 10 minutes until browned and fragrant, shaking or stirring often. Cool completely. Grind in a mortar and pestle or spice grinder until a coarse powder, like cornmeal.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 4
method.body: In a medium saucepan, combine the pork and 1 tablespoon water. Cook on medium 5 minutes, until pork is cooked through but not browned, breaking up into small bits and stirring often. Remove from heat.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Step 3

method.body: To the pork, add the shallot, lime juice, fish sauce and chile. Stir to combine. Add the cilantro and scallions.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Just before serving, stir in half of the toasted ground rice. Taste and add remaining rice for more crunch, if desired. Fold in the mint. Transfer to a serving dish. Garnish with more mint, if desired. Serve with cabbage wedges, long beans, cucumbers and lettuce leaves.

method.title: Step 9
method.body: If you can’t find ground Thai chiles, you can finely grind crushed red pepper flakes in a spice grinder. Just be careful when removing the lid of your spice grinder, as the potent dust cloud that floats up can be cough inducing.

selected.title: Pork Laab
selected.author: Sherry Rujikarn
ingredients: ½–1½ tsp ground red chiles, to taste
¼ cup cilantro leaves and stems, chopped
¼ cup fresh mint leaves, roughly torn, plus more for serving
Cabbage wedges, long beans, cucumber and lettuce, for serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Toast the rice in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 10 minutes, until browned and fragrant. Cool and grind to a coarse powder.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Cook the pork with 1 tablespoon of water over medium heat for about 5 minutes, breaking it into small pieces without browning.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Remove from the heat and mix in the shallot, lime juice, fish sauce, chile, cilantro and scallions.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Just before serving, stir in half the toasted rice, adding more for extra crunch. Fold in the mint and serve with the vegetables.

items.title: Receta casera de salsa de tomate Cherry

expectedSnapshot.title: Receta casera de salsa de tomate Cherry
expectedSnapshot.author: Sal al gusto
ingredients: 3 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra
Hierbas aromáticas (albahaca, orégano o tomillo, al gusto)
Pelamos y picamos la cebolla en trozos pequeños, hacemos lo mismo con el diente de ajo. Reservamos.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: En una sartén amplia añadimos un buen chorro de aceite de oliva virgen extra y salteamos los tomates para que pillen un toque dorado y suelten parte de su jugo a la sartén. Sacamos de la sartén y luego sofreímos la cebolla y un diente de ajo a fuego medio, hasta que quede transparente y ligeramente dorada.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Incorporamos los tomates Cherry salteados y removemos bien para que se mezclen con la cebolla. Cocinamos a fuego medio durante unos 15 minutos, removiendo de vez en cuando para evitar que se peguen. Verás cómo van soltando todo su jugo.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Añadimos la sal, una pizca de azúcar (si lo consideramos necesario) y las hierbas aromáticas que más nos gusten (en nuestro caso hemos optado por orégano, pero la albahaca fresca le va genial, un toque italiano para tu salsa). Tapamos la sartén y dejamos cocinar otros 10 minutos a fuego suave.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Cuando los tomates estén bien blandos y la salsa haya reducido suele quedar bastante blandita, podemos triturar con una batidora o dejarla así, a mi me gusta más la última opción, una salsa más rústica, podemos simplemente aplastar los tomates con una cuchara de madera. Pero si tienes niños en casa y están acostumbrados a la textura fina, batidora.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Probamos y rectificamos de sal o azúcar si hace falta. Si la salsa nos queda muy líquida, podemos dejarla unos minutos más al fuego para que espese. Si, por el contrario, está demasiado densa, añadimos un chorrito de agua o caldo.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Guardamos la salsa en un tarro de cristal esterilizado si queremos conservarla unos días, o la usamos directamente como base para pasta, pizzas, carnes o lo que se nos ocurra.

expectedSnapshot.title: Recipes
expectedSnapshot.author: Sarah DiGregorio
ingredients: 1 ½ to 2 pounds boneless, skinless chicken thighs
3 (4-ounce) cans hot or mild chopped green chiles (not drained)
1 bell pepper, sliced into ¼-inch wide strips
2 tablespoons Tajín seasoning, plus more for serving
Rice, tortillas or tortilla chips, for serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Combine the chicken, beans, green chiles, bell pepper, garlic, Tajín, oil, onion powder and cumin in a 6- to 8-quart slow cooker. Season with 1½ teaspoons salt. Cook on low until the chicken is tender and the pepper is soft, 4 hours.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Break the chicken into large chunks using two forks. Serve topped with queso fresco and more Tajin to taste.

expectedSnapshot.title: ROASTED BEETROOT & FETA SOUP
expectedSnapshot.author: @ADAMHOAD_
ingredients: 800ml chicken bone broth or vegetable stock

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Preheat the oven to 180°C. Add the beetroot, carrots, cauliflower, onions, and garlic to a large oven dish or tray. Season with salt, pepper, cumin, and coriander.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Place into the preheated oven and roast for 40 to 45 minutes until soft and lightly caramelised.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Transfer the roasted vegetables to a blender. Add the stock/broth, silken tofu, and juice from the lemon.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Blend until completely smooth.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Serve hot and crumble some feta over the top.

method.body: Roast veg and chickpeas:

method.title: Step 15
method.body: Preheat oven to 200°C/400°F. Mix the seasoning/spices in a bowl.

method.title: Step 16
method.body: Add the chickpeas, cauliflower and Brussels sprouts to the baking pan (lined with parchment paper if it’s not non-stick). Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle over the seasoning. Toss to coat. Bake for around 40 minutes, until lightly golden. I like to give it a toss around the 30 minute mark.

method.title: Step 17
method.body: Whilst it’s baking, mix together the yogurt sauce ingredients in a bowl.

method.title: Step 18
method.body: Add the yogurt to the base of the bowls and top with the roasted veg and chickpeas. Sprinkle with sesame seeds, pomegranate and parsley. Enjoy!

selected.title: Roasted spiced crunchy chickpeas, cauliflower and brussels sprouts over creamy cucumber yogurt sauce.
ingredients: 1 cup plain coconut or soy Greek-style yogurt
1 can chickpeas (420 g), drained, rinsed and dried

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Preheat the oven to 200°C and mix the spices in a bowl.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Put the chickpeas, cauliflower and Brussels sprouts on a lined baking tray. Drizzle with olive oil, add the seasoning and toss to coat.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Roast for about 40 minutes, tossing after around 30 minutes.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Mix the cucumber-yogurt ingredients.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Spread the yogurt sauce in the bowls, top with the roasted vegetables and chickpeas, and finish with parsley, pomegranate and sesame seeds.

items.title: Roasted Sweet Potato Stacks with Chipotle Sauce

expectedSnapshot.title: Roasted Sweet Potato Stacks with Chipotle Sauce
ingredients: 4 medium sweet potatoes, washed and cut in half lengthwise
1 onion, thinly sliced (or 1 teaspoon onion powder)
1 heaping tablespoon taco seasoning (about half a packet, I use Siete mild)
1/2 cup salsa (I like Siete casera salsa roja, or Mateo’s)
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt (depends on taco seasoning, adjust to taste)
Something crunchy, like Mexican chili crisp or crushed pistachios

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Cook Mode Prevent your screen from going dark

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Prep Sweet Potatoes: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees. Brush or drizzle all sides of the sweet potatoes with oil; place face-down on a sheet pan. Sprinkle top side with salt.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Roast Sweet Potatoes: Bake for 20 minutes. Use a spatula to gently lift one and check for doneness (you want to preserve that caramelized surface area). Bake for an additional 10-15 minutes as needed to cook through and caramelize the bottom side.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Cook Chicken: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add the onion; sauté until soft and fragrant. Add chicken and taco seasoning; brown, breaking up with a spatula, until cooked through.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Sauce Chicken: Add salsa and salt to the chicken; sauté for another 1-2 minutes. Cover to keep warm until you’re ready to serve.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Sauce: Whisk all sauce ingredients until smooth. Taste and adjust.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Toppings: Cut avocado, cilantro, onions, nuts or seeds for topping.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Serve: I stack in this order: sweet potato, then sauce, then chicken, then toppings. Possibly more sauce on top. So, so good.

items.title: Roasted Tomato and White Bean Stew

expectedSnapshot.title: Roasted Tomato and White Bean Stew
expectedSnapshot.author: Colu Henry
ingredients: 1/2cup roughly chopped Italian parsley leaves and tender stems
1/4cup olive oil, plus 2 tablespoons and more for drizzling (optional)
2(15-ounce) cans white beans (such as butter or cannellini), rinsed
11/2cups vegetable or chicken broth, or water

method.title: Step 1
method.body: This hearty, flexible stew comes together with pantry ingredients and delivers layers of flavors. Cherry tomatoes, roasted in a generous glug of olive oil to amplify their sweetness, lend a welcome brightness to this otherwise rich dish. Onion, garlic and red-pepper flakes form the backbone of this dish, to which white beans and broth are added, then simmered until thick. While this stew is lovely on its own, you could also add wilt-able greens such as kale, escarole or Swiss chard at the end, and toasted bread crumbs on top. The dish is vegan as written, but should you choose to top your bowl with a showering of grated pecorino or Parmesan, it would most likely work well in your favor.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 1⁄2cup roughly chopped Italian parsley leaves and tender stems

method.title: Step 3
method.body: 2teaspoons lemon zest (from 1 large lemon)

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 2(10-ounce) containers cherry or grape tomatoes

method.title: Step 5
method.body: 1⁄4cup olive oil, plus 2 tablespoons and more for drizzling (optional)

method.title: Step 6
method.body: 1tablespoon fresh thyme leaves

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Kosher salt and black pepper

method.title: Step 8
method.body: 1medium yellow onion, thinly sliced

method.title: Step 9
method.body: 3large garlic cloves, thinly sliced

method.title: Step 10
method.body: 1⁄2teaspoon red-pepper flakes

method.title: Step 11
method.body: 2(15-ounce) cans white beans (such as butter or cannellini), rinsed

method.title: Step 12
method.body: 11⁄2cups vegetable or chicken broth, or water

method.title: Step 13
method.body: Flaky salt, for serving (optional)

method.title: Step 14
method.body: Toasted bread, for serving

method.title: Step 15
method.body: Add ingredients to Grocery List

method.title: Step 16
method.body: Step 1

method.title: Step 17
method.body: Heat the oven to 425 degrees. In a small bowl, gently toss together the parsley and lemon zest with your hands until well combined; set aside.

method.title: Step 18
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 19
method.body: In a large baking dish or on a sheet pan, toss the tomatoes with 1⁄4 cup oil and thyme; season well with salt and pepper. Roast tomatoes until they have collapsed and begin to turn golden around the edges, 20 to 25 minutes.

method.title: Step 20
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 21
method.body: When the tomatoes are almost done roasting, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large (12-inch), deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium. Add the onion, garlic and red-pepper flakes and cook until the onion is softened and the garlic is fragrant, 4 to 5 minutes. Stir in the rinsed beans and broth and bring to a simmer. With the back of a spoon or spatula, gently smash about 1⁄2 cup of the beans so they slightly thicken the broth. If you want a thicker stew, crush some more of the beans. Season with salt and pepper.

method.title: Step 22
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 23
method.body: When the tomatoes are finished roasting, add them directly to the stew along with any juices that have been released. Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes more so the flavors become friendly; season to taste with salt.

method.title: Step 24
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 25
method.body: Ladle into shallow bowls. Top each serving with some of the lemon-parsley mixture and drizzle with more olive oil, and season with flaky salt, if you like. Serve with toasted bread.

items.title: Rosemary White Beans With Frizzled Onions and Tomato

expectedSnapshot.title: Rosemary White Beans With Frizzled Onions and Tomato
expectedSnapshot.author: Melissa Clark
ingredients: 1large white onion, halved and thinly sliced into half moons
2teaspoons minced fresh rosemary, or 1/2 teaspoon dried rosemary
2(15-ounce) cans white beans, such as cannellini or butter beans (preferably canned with salt), drained and rinsed
1cup chopped fresh parsley leaves and tender stems, more for garnish

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Add ingredients to Grocery List

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Nutritional Information

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Step 1

method.title: Step 4
method.body: In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons oil until it shimmers over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until well browned all over, 7 to 10 minutes. Reduce heat to medium, transfer half of the onions to a plate and season lightly with salt.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Add remaining 6 tablespoons oil, the garlic, rosemary, red-pepper flakes and a pinch of salt to the onions in the skillet. Cook until garlic is pale gold at the edges (don’t let the garlic turn brown), 2 to 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Add beans, chopped tomatoes, 1⁄2 cup of water and 1 teaspoon salt to skillet; stir until beans are well coated with sauce. Bring to a simmer over medium-low heat and cook until broth thickens, stirring occasionally, about 10 to 15 minutes.

method.title: Step 9
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 10
method.body: Stir in lemon zest and parsley, and taste, adding more salt if needed. Garnish with reserved onions, more parsley, olive oil and red-pepper flakes, if you’d like. The beans thicken as they cool, but you can add more water to make them brothier if you like.

selected.title: Rosemary White Beans With Frizzled Onions and Tomato
selected.author: Melissa Clark
ingredients: 1large white onion, halved and thinly sliced into half moons
2teaspoons minced fresh rosemary, or 1/2 teaspoon dried rosemary
2(15-ounce) cans white beans, such as cannellini or butter beans (preferably canned with salt), drained and rinsed
1cup chopped fresh parsley leaves and tender stems, more for garnish

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Add ingredients to Grocery List

method.title: Step 2
method.body: In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons oil until it shimmers over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until well browned all over, 7 to 10 minutes. Reduce heat to medium, transfer half of the onions to a plate and season lightly with salt.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Add remaining 6 tablespoons oil, the garlic, rosemary, red-pepper flakes and a pinch of salt to the onions in the skillet. Cook until garlic is pale gold at the edges (don’t let the garlic turn brown), 2 to 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Add beans, chopped tomatoes, 1⁄2 cup of water and 1 teaspoon salt to skillet; stir until beans are well coated with sauce. Bring to a simmer over medium-low heat and cook until broth thickens, stirring occasionally, about 10 to 15 minutes.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Stir in lemon zest and parsley, and taste, adding more salt if needed. Garnish with reserved onions, more parsley, olive oil and red-pepper flakes, if you’d like. The beans thicken as they cool, but you can add more water to make them brothier if you like.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 44g Protein

method.title: Step 3
method.body: 65g Carbs

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 15g Fat

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Cook once, eat ten times 🤝

method.title: Step 6
method.body: 1.5 Tbsp garam masala

method.title: Step 7
method.body: 1 Tbsp paprika

method.title: Step 8
method.body: 2 tsp spicy curry powder

method.title: Step 9
method.body: 1 tsp black pepper

method.title: Step 10
method.body: 1⁄2 tsp cayenne

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Dash of ground cardamom

method.title: Step 12
method.body: 1.5 Tbsp kosher salt

selected.title: ROTISSERIE BUTTER CHICKEN
ingredients: 2 whole rotisserie chickens, skin removed, about 1200 g meat

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Remove the skin from the rotisserie chickens and shred or chop the meat.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Melt the butter in a large pan. Add the tomato paste, garlic paste and ginger paste and cook until fragrant.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Add the tomatoes, cashews, water and spice blend. Simmer until the cashews soften, then blend until smooth.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Return the sauce to the pan. Stir in the yogurt, evaporated milk and honey, then add the chicken and heat through.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Cook the basmati rice. Divide the rice and butter chicken among ten portions and garnish with cilantro and garam masala.

expectedSnapshot.title: Saag Shrimp
expectedSnapshot.author: Adapted by Yewande Komolafe
ingredients: 1pound peeled, deveined large shrimp, tails off
3tablespoons ghee or neutral oil, such as grapeseed or safflower
1(1-inch) piece fresh ginger, scrubbed and grated
1/2cup fresh cilantro leaves and stems, chopped
1pound fresh baby spinach, roughly chopped using a sharp knife or by pulsing in a food processor
1/2cup heavy cream or unsweetened coconut cream
Steamed rice, naan, green chile chutney, tamarind chutney and fresh kachumbari, or a combination, for serving
Toss the shrimp with 2 teaspoons of the spice blend and a light sprinkle of salt. Set aside.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Make the spice blend: In a small bowl, combine turmeric, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne, garlic powder and onion powder.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Heat a large shallow skillet over medium-high. Add the ghee and cumin seeds, and toast until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add the onion, cook until softened and translucent, stirring frequently, 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Add the remaining spice blend, stir and toast until fragrant, 30 seconds. Add the ginger, garlic and tomato paste. Stir and cook until the tomato paste deepens in color, about 1 minute. Reserve 1 tablespoon of the chopped tomato for garnish. Add the remaining chopped tomato, about half of the chopped cilantro and 1⁄2 cup water. Increase the heat to high and bring the sauce to a simmer. Cook the sauce, stirring until it thickens and reduces (about three-fourths of the original volume), 3 to 5 minutes. Season with salt.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Add the shrimp to the skillet all at once and stir to coat with the sauce. Add the spinach in two batches, wilting the first batch before adding the second, and season with salt. Cook on high heat, stirring occasionally, until spinach is softened, shrimp is cooked through and most of the liquid has evaporated, about 4 to 5 minutes. Stir in the heavy cream and adjust the seasoning to taste with more salt, if necessary.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Step 6

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Remove from heat and garnish with the reserved chopped tomatoes and the cilantro. Serve over steamed rice and warm, pillowy naan. Top the saag with green chile chutney, tamarind chutney or fresh kachumbari for a lovely pop of acid and heat.

items.title: Salmón de Noruega con salsa de miel y naranja.

expectedSnapshot.title: Salmón de Noruega con salsa de miel y naranja.
expectedSnapshot.author: Zumo de media naranja
ingredients: 3/4 lomos de Salmón de Noruega con piel

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Mezcla la soja, miel y el zumo de naranja.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Haz el Salmón de Noruega en una sartén un poco engrasada, lo ideal es no hacer el Salmón del todo ya que se va terminar de hacer más tarde junto con la salsa.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Retira el Salmón, en la misma sartén, añade el ajo picado y la salsa, deja a fuego alto y removiendo de vez en cuando hasta que veas que espesa.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: En ese momento, añade el Salmón de Noruega y ve poniéndole la salsa por encima durante un par de minutos.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Sirve con un poco de sésamo por encima y unas setas a la plancha de guarnición.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Espero que os guste tanto como a mí, gracias por todos vuestros comentarios y qué disfrutéis del día.

items.title: Salmon Green Beans in Red Pepper Sauce

expectedSnapshot.title: Salmon Green Beans in Red Pepper Sauce
expectedSnapshot.author: Yewande Komolafe
ingredients: 1 small red onion, peeled and chopped (about 1 cup)
1 (16-ounce) jar roasted red bell peppers, drained and chopped (about 1 1/2 cups)
1 Scotch bonnet or habanero chile, seeds in or out
4 salmon fillets (about 6 ounces each), skin on or off
8 ounces green beans, trimmed and cut into 2-inch pieces (about 2 cups)

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Prepare the red pepper sauce: Using a food processor or blender, coarsely chop the onion, roasted red peppers, Scotch bonnet and garlic by pulsing them together.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Heat a large (10-inch) cast iron or nonstick skillet over high and add 2 tablespoons oil.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Place the salmon fillets in the hot oil, skin side down, season lightly with salt and pepper and cook without moving until the contact side is gently browned, about 5 minutes. Move the salmon to a plate, setting it skin side up, and set aside.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Turn the heat down to medium-high, add 1 tablespoon oil to the skillet, the green beans, anchovies and miso. Cook, stirring frequently, mashing the anchovies with the spatula until miso is broken up and anchovies are dissolved. Add 1⁄2 cup water to the skillet and bring to a simmer. Cook until green beans are just wrinkly, 4 to 6 minutes.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Reduce heat to medium, pour the red pepper sauce into the skillet. Stir in 1⁄2 cup water and the remaining 2 tablespoons oil. Simmer the sauce, stirring frequently until slightly reduced and the liquid and oil collect toward the center of the skillet, 5 to 6 minutes. Taste for seasoning and adjust with additional salt and pepper if necessary.

method.title: Step 9
method.body: Step 6

method.title: Step 10
method.body: Using a wooden spoon, create divots in the sauce and green beans. Nestle the fillets in the divots, skin side up. Cook until the fish is fork-tender, the sauce reduced and the green beans are softened, 6 to 8 minutes.

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Step 7

method.title: Step 12
method.body: Garnish with scallions and serve fish, green beans and sauce immediately over rice, with lemon wedges for squeezing.

method.title: Step 13
method.body: Ajuste kcal por ración aprox.

expectedSnapshot.title: Sheet-Pan Charred Meat Pitas
ingredients: Neutral oil, for greasing the pan
1/4cup chopped parsley leaves and stems, plus more for serving
1pound ground beef (preferably 80 percent lean), or ground lamb
Kosher salt, such as Diamond Crystal
Halved cherry tomatoes, sliced red onions, radish wedges or sliced cucumbers

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Using a small sharp knife, gently cut a slit around the edges of each pita, then gently pull apart to split it into two. Repeat to end up with 8 pita halves (you may need additional pitas later). Lightly grease a large baking sheet.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Make the kofta mixture by adding the onion, parsley, garlic, coriander, cumin, allspice and chile flakes to a food processor or mini chopper and pulsing until the onion, parsley and garlic appear very finely chopped. (Alternatively, finely chop onion and parsley and finely grate the garlic.) Transfer to a medium bowl, add beef and 2 teaspoons salt, and mix very well to evenly combine.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Using a 1⁄4-cup measure, firmly scoop a portion of the kofta mixture against the side of the bowl and transfer to the center of the inner side of each pita. Spread the mixture into a thin, even layer from edge to edge of the pita. Transfer up to 8 pitas to the prepared baking sheet. (It’s OK if the edges of the pitas slightly overlap.) If there is extra kofta mixture left over, repeat the process with additional pita halves. Set the additional pitas aside to broil after the first batch is done.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Place oven rack in upper third of the oven; heat to high broil. Transfer baking sheet to oven and broil, rotating the baking sheet halfway through, until meat is nicely cooked and charred in spots, 5 to 10 minutes.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 9
method.body: Serve the pitas topped with a dollop of hummus, labneh or toum, plus more parsley, a combination of whichever veggies you choose and a squeeze of lemon. Drizzle with pomegranate molasses if desired

method.title: Step 10
method.body: Kofta mixture (without salt) can be made up to 2 days in advance and stored in an airtight container in the fridge or frozen for up to 3 months; thaw in the refrigerator overnight and season with salt before using. The broiled pitas can be stored between sheets of parchment paper in a zip-top freezer bag and frozen for up to 3 months. Reheat from frozen in a 375-degree oven until warmed through.

expectedSnapshot.title: Sheet-Pan Malai Chicken and Potatoes
expectedSnapshot.author: Zaynab Issa
ingredients: 1/3cup cilantro leaves and tender stems, torn to fit the blender, plus more cilantro for serving if desired
1 to 2serrano chiles, depending on your heat tolerance

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Cook Time

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 40 minutes

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Yield:

method.title: Step 4
method.body: For the Chicken and Potatoes

method.title: Step 5
method.body: 11⁄2 pounds fingerling or baby potatoes

method.title: Step 6
method.body: 1medium yellow onion

method.title: Step 7
method.body: 1tablespoon melted ghee or olive oil

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Kosher salt (such as Diamond Crystal)

method.title: Step 9
method.body: 4bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs (about 11⁄2 pounds total)

method.title: Step 10
method.body: 1tablespoon fresh lemon juice

method.title: Step 11
method.body: 2tablespoons heavy cream

method.title: Step 12
method.body: For the Marinade

method.title: Step 13
method.body: 1⁄4cup full-fat plain yogurt

method.title: Step 14
method.body: 1⁄3cup cilantro leaves and tender stems, torn to fit the blender, plus more cilantro for serving if desired

method.title: Step 15
method.body: 5garlic cloves

method.title: Step 16
method.body: 1(11⁄2-inch) piece fresh ginger, sliced

method.title: Step 17
method.body: 1 to 2serrano chiles, depending on your heat tolerance

method.title: Step 18
method.body: 1tablespoon ghee or olive oil

method.title: Step 19
method.body: 11⁄2 teaspoons garam masala

method.title: Step 20
method.body: Step 1

method.title: Step 21
method.body: Arrange the oven rack in the center of the oven and heat to 425 degrees.

method.title: Step 22
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 23
method.body: Start the chicken and potatoes: As the oven heats, halve potatoes lengthwise (quarter and cut any larger ones). Halve the onion lengthwise and cut each half into quarters. Transfer potatoes and onion to a sheet pan, add the ghee and season with 11⁄2 teaspoons salt. Transfer to the preheated oven, and roast for 10 minutes while you prepare the marinade.

method.title: Step 24
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 25
method.body: Make the marinade: Using a blender, mini food processor or immersion blender, combine the yogurt, cilantro, garlic, ginger, chiles, ghee, garam masala and 11⁄2 teaspoons salt. Blend until the mixture is mostly smooth. If needed, add a tablespoon of water to help the blender along. Transfer the marinade to a medium bowl or resealable bag, and add the chicken. Massage marinade into chicken and let marinate while the potatoes cook (see Tip).

method.title: Step 26
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 27
method.body: When the vegetables have roasted for 10 minutes, remove the pan from the oven and nestle chicken on top, skin side up, scraping any excess marinade from the chicken skin onto the vegetables. Return sheet pan to the oven and adjust heat to 450 degrees. Roast until the chicken and potatoes are cooked through and the chicken skin is browned and crisp, 30 to 35 minutes. (If the potatoes aren’t fork-tender yet, remove the chicken from the pan and continue to roast the potatoes for another 5 to 10 minutes.)

method.title: Step 28
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 29
method.body: Carefully transfer the chicken to a plate, leaving the vegetables in the pan. Add the lemon juice, cream and 3 tablespoons hot water to the sheet pan and toss with a metal spatula, carefully scraping up any browned bits, until the potatoes are coated in sauce. Transfer chicken back over top and serve immediately, garnished with more cilantro if you like.

method.title: Step 30
method.body: If you like, the chicken can marinate for up to 2 hours on the counter, or ideally, overnight in the refrigerator. (If marinating overnight, remove the chicken from the fridge at least 30 minutes before roasting to ensure it cooks evenly.)

selected.title: Sheet-Pan Malai Chicken and Potatoes
selected.author: Zaynab Issa
ingredients: 1 tbsp melted ghee or olive oil
4 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs (about 1½ lb total)
⅓ cup cilantro leaves and tender stems, plus more for serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Heat the oven to 220°C.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Halve the potatoes and cut the onion into wedges. Put them on a sheet pan with the ghee and 1½ teaspoons salt and roast for 10 minutes.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Blend the yogurt, cilantro, garlic, ginger, chiles, ghee, garam masala and 1½ teaspoons salt. Coat the chicken with the marinade.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Place the chicken skin-side up over the potatoes and onion. Increase the oven to 230°C and roast for 30–35 minutes, until the chicken is cooked and crisp.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Transfer the chicken to a plate. Add the lemon juice, cream and 3 tablespoons hot water to the vegetables, scraping up the browned bits. Return the chicken and serve.

expectedSnapshot.title: Slow-Cooker Sunday Sauce
ingredients: 2pounds boneless pork shoulder, cut into 3-inch chunks
1/4cup extra-virgin olive oil, plus more as needed
1pound hot or sweet Italian sausage, or a combination
11/2pounds tubular or long noodles, like rigatoni or spaghetti
Grated Parmesan or pecorino, for serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Step 1

method.title: Step 2
method.body: In a 6- to 8-quart slow cooker, add the crushed tomatoes and basil sprigs. Season the pork shoulder all over with salt and pepper.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Step 2

method.title: Step 4
method.body: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons oil over medium-high. Working in batches if necessary, cook the pork until browned on two sides, 8 to 10 minutes total, adding more oil if the pan looks dry. Transfer pieces to the slow cooker as they finish. Add the sausages to the pot and cook until browned, 4 to 6 minutes total. Transfer to the slow cooker.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons oil, if needed, and the onion and garlic. Season with salt and pepper, and cook until softened, 2 to 4 minutes. Add the red wine, stirring to scrape up the browned bits on the bottom of the pot, and cook until the wine is nearly evaporated, 2 to 4 minutes. Scrape the onion-wine mixture into the slow cooker, and stir to combine. Add the meatballs on top. (It’s OK if they’re sticking out of the sauce.) Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours, until the pork shoulder falls apart when shredded with a fork.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 8
method.body: When you’re ready to eat, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente. While the pasta boils, slice the sausage and shred the pork shoulder on a cutting board, and leave it there. When the pasta is al dente, reserve 1⁄2 cup pasta water, then drain and return the pasta to the pot. Over medium heat, toss the pasta with enough sauce to coat (about 4 cups), adding pasta water as needed until the sauce clings to the pasta. (Some meatballs might end up in the pasta pot; that’s OK.) Return the sausage and pork shoulder to the remaining sauce in the slow cooker.

method.title: Step 9
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 10
method.body: Divide pasta between bowls, then top with a bit of each meat. Pass the Parmesan and extra meat and sauce at the table.

expectedSnapshot.title: Sopa de verduras y albondiguitas🥹🥰👏🏻
expectedSnapshot.author: Una cebolla
ingredients: Medio vaso de salsa de tomate casera o tomate frito casero
Un litro de agua aproximadamente. Se puede añadir más.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Ponemos un buen chorrito de aceite en una olla y lo calentamos. Añadimos la cebolla picada y cuando esté transparente el tomate, cúrcuma y romero.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Rehogamos e incorporamos el resto de verduras bien picaditas. Sofreímos a fuego medio hasta que estén pochaditas.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Añadimos el agua y dejamos cocinar a fuego medio treinta minutos.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Sacamos unas verduras y las trituramos para darle más cuerpo al caldo. Las añadimos.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Formamos las albondiguitas picando la pechuga y formándolas con las manos untadas con aceite. Le podéis añadir algunas especias o lo que queráis. En realidad no deja de ser pollo, pero con otra forma🥰

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Las incorporamos a la olla y cocinamos 20 minutos más esta vez a fuego fuerte.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Y lista! Podéis también poner algo de pasta si queréis para que sea más completa :)

expectedSnapshot.title: SOUTZOUKÁKIA Baked Meatballs
expectedSnapshot.author: handful of mint leaves
ingredients: 2 chicken stock cubes, dissolved in 500ml (18fl oz) hot water

1 Start by preparing the meatballs. Blitz the bread pieces in a food processor for about a minute to make fresh breadcrumbs (don’t worry if a few chunkier bits remain). Tip the breadcrumbs into a large mixing bowl.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: 2 Add the garlic, mint leaves, red onion, cumin, cinnamon and chilli flakes to the food processor. Blitz until a paste forms. Add this to the bowl of breadcrumbs, along with the minced meats, egg and a generous seasoning of salt and pepper (I’ve given my recommended amounts). Mix by hand, wearing disposable gloves if you wish, to ensure the seasonings and breadcrumbs are well incorporated into the meat.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 3 Set up 3 plates. Put the flour on one; the second is for placing the prepared meatballs; and line the third with kitchen paper, ready for absorbing excess oil from the fried meatballs. Take a heaped tablespoon of the meatball mixture and shape into an oval. Dredge in the flour and set aside on the second plate. Repeat with the rest to make about 20 meatballs.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: 4 Add the sunflower oil to a large frying pan and place over a high heat. To check the oil is hot enough, stick the tip of a wooden spoon in the pan to see if the oil bubbles up around it. Add the meatballs to the pan, one at a time, without overcrowding it; if they don’t all fit at once, cook them in batches. Brown the meatballs for 2–3 minutes on both sides.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 5 Remove the meatballs from the frying pan and place on the plate lined with kitchen paper. 6 Use the same pan to make the sauce. Reserve 2 tablespoons of the cooking oil and drain off the rest. Place the pan over a medium heat, add the onion and sugar, and sauté for 2 minutes, stirring regularly, until the onion caramelizes. Stir in the tomatoes, cinnamon, bay leaves and prepared chicken stock. Lower the heat and simmer for 10–12 minutes until the sauce reduces.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: 7 Meanwhile preheat the oven to 170°C fan (375°F), Gas Mark 5. Put the meatballs in a large roasting tray and pour over the sauce – make sure they are all covered. Bake for 40 minutes.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: 8 Serve warm with basmati rice or pitta bread to soak up all the delicious sauce.
