expectedSnapshot.title: SPANAKÓRIZO Greek-style Spinach Risotto
ingredients: 1 vegetable stock cube, dissolved in 500ml (18fl oz) hot water
crumbled feta cheese, to serve (about 30g/1oz per serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: 1 Pour the oil into a large saucepan and place over a medium heat. After a minute, add the chopped spring onions and herbs and stir through the oil. Sauté for 2–3 minutes until softened, stirring regularly. about 2–3 minutes or until the onion has softened, then add the carrots and cook for a further 2–3 minutes.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 2 Wash the rice, rinsing until the water runs clear, then drain. Add the rice and prepared vegetable stock to the pan, stir to combine, then cover with a lid and turn down the heat to low. Simmer for 15 minutes, until two-thirds of the liquid has been absorbed.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: 3 Next, incorporate the spinach. I add this in batches, stirring in a large handful of spinach and allowing it to wilt before adding the next batch. Repeat this until all of the spinach has been added to the rice, cover and cook over a low heat until all the liquid has fully been absorbed. This will take a further 15 minutes.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 4 Once the rice has become silky in texture, pour in the lemon juice and season generously with salt and pepper. Stir to combine and serve the spanakórizo with crumbled feta on top.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: For the herb salad, slice the cucumber into thin half moons, pick the coriander leaves and finely slice the green tops of the spring onions. Combine in a bowl. Chill in iced water if you want more crunch.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Add the garlic, gochujang, Korean chilli flakes, dark soy, rice vinegar, sugar and the white parts of the spring onions to a blender. Blitz until smooth and set aside.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Heat a large frying pan over high heat. Add the oil, then the chicken mince. Press it flat and let it brown deeply on one side before breaking it up. Cook through, then pour in the chilli sauce. Stir and let it bubble for 2–3 minutes until thick, sticky and fully coating the chicken.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Cook the noodles according to pack instructions. Drain and toss immediately with the miso peanut sauce and a splash of warm noodle water until glossy and well coated.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Divide the saucy noodles between bowls. Top with the spicy chicken and the herb salad. Finish with extra miso peanut sauce if you like.

expectedSnapshot.title: SPICY PEANUT PRAWN NOODLES
expectedSnapshot.author: Fit Foodie Lulu
ingredients: 1 tsp each garlic powder and onion powder; 1/2 tsp each smoked paprika and salt
1/2 cup spring onions, thinly sliced (whites and greens separated)
4 cups Chinese broccoli, stems and leaves separated and chopped

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Cook the noodles according to packet instructions. If using frozen, loosen in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain well. Blend all sauce ingredients until smooth and creamy. Set aside.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Season prawns with garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika and salt and a light drizzle of oil. Heat a large deep pan over high heat. Sear prawns for 1–2 minutes per side until golden and just cooked through (work in 2 batches to avoid overcrowding). Add light butter, toss to coat, then finish with chopped coriander. Remove and set aside.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: In the same pan, sauté garlic and broccoli stems for 3 minutes. Add Chinese broccoli leaves and stir-fry for a further 1–2 minutes until just tender and vibrant.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Add the noodles and pour in the spicy peanut sauce. Toss until everything is glossy and well coated.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Return prawns to the pan, add spring onions, and toss through. Serve immediately while hot and saucy, finished with a drizzle of chilli oil. See less

items.title: Spicy Sardine Pasta With Leeks and Lemon

expectedSnapshot.title: Spicy Sardine Pasta With Leeks and Lemon
expectedSnapshot.author: Ashley Lonsdale
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
2 (4-ounce) tins sardines packed in olive oil
2 medium leeks, trimmed, white and pale green parts only, halved lengthwise, thinly sliced and rinsed
1/4 teaspoon finely grated lemon zest plus two tablespoons lemon juice

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil over high heat.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Heat a large skillet over medium-high. Pour the olive oil from the sardine tins into the skillet. (You should get about 1⁄4 cup, but you can supplement as needed with additional olive oil.) Add the leeks and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and golden brown, 7 to 9 minutes. Add the garlic and chile and stir until fragrant, about 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Step 3

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Add the pasta to the large pot and cook, stirring occasionally to ensure the strands don’t stick together, for 1 minute less than the recommended cooking time on the package directions.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Step 4

method.title: Step 6
method.body: While the pasta cooks, lower heat under the skillet to medium. Add the sardines to the skillet and mash into bits with a spoon. Stir in the lemon juice, zest and capers.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Step 5

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Using tongs, transfer the pasta to the skillet along with 1 cup pasta water. Finish cooking the pasta in the sauce for about 1 minute, turning and tossing with tongs, until all but a small amount of loose sauce is absorbed, adding splashes of additional pasta water if needed. Garnish the pasta with chives, a drizzle of olive oil and freshly cracked pepper.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: 1 Pour the oil into a large saucepan and place over a high heat. After a minute, add the shallots and cover the pan with a lid. Leave the shallots to soften and caramelize for 5 minutes, shaking the pan to turn them around or flipping them around using tongs (be careful with the hot oil), then reduce the heat to medium and cover the pan again. Once the shallots are golden brown, use a slotted spoon to transfer them to a bowl.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 2 Add the beef chunks to the pan, in 2 batches if necessary so that the pan is not overcrowded. Brown the beef chunks for about 5 minutes, turning to colour on both sides.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: 3 Add the tomato purée and stir through to coat the beef. Then add the garlic, chopped tomatoes, passata, sugar, red wine, crumbled stock cube, spices, oregano and bay leaves. Season with a generous amount of salt and pepper, stir to combine then cover the pan with a lid and reduce the heat to low.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 4 Simmer the stifado for a total of 3 hours, stirring occasionally throughout. The sauce will reduce, and the beef will become tender. After 21⁄2 hours, add the caramelized shallots back into the pan and stir them through the sauce, along with the butter. Leave the stew to simmer, uncovered, for the final 30 minutes, to reduce the sauce even further.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: 5 When ready, remove the bay leaves and whole cloves. Serve the stifado with long-grain rice.

method.title: Step 1

Leave the shallots to soften and caramelize for 5 minutes, shaking the pan to turn them around or flipping them around using tongs (be careful with the hot oil), then reduce the heat to medium and cover the pan again. Once the shallots are golden brown, use a slotted spoon to transfer them to a bowl.

method.title: Step 2

method.title: Step 4

method.title: Step 5
method.body: When ready, remove the bay leaves and whole cloves. Serve the stifado with long-grain rice.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: 1 Pour the oil into a frying pan and place over a medium–high heat. After a minute, add the onion,garlic and herbs, and stir through. Cook for 2 minutes until softened.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: 2 Add the tomatoes along with the balsamic vinegar glaze and stir through the sautéed onion–garlic mixture. Season to taste and cook for a further 3 minutes until the sauce is reduced and the tomatoes have darkened in colour.

method.title: Step 3

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 4 Remove the pan from the heat and crumble in the feta.

method.title: Step 5

items.title: Sweet potato filled with beef and sriracha sauce

expectedSnapshot.title: Sweet potato filled with beef and sriracha sauce
ingredients: Plus, the creamy sriracha sauce is SO good you’ll want to put it on everything.
If you are watching your carbs, you can sub in spaghetti squash, eggplant, or even zucchini!

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Start by slow roasting 6 small sweet potatoes at 350°F for 75 mins. Time depends on thickness. You can always roast on higher heat (400F for 50 mins) but I prefer slow roasting.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Cook up 1 lb lean ground beef (I used 93/7 to be more calorically friendly) in 1 tsp olive or avocado oil. Season the beef with salt, pepper, 1 tbsp chipotle powder, 1tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp oregano or thyme, and 1 tbsp sweet paprika. Mince it down with a spatula so its in small pieces. I like to get these browned up really nicely, almost crispy in a sense.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: 3.When your sweet potato are done, let them cool for a minute or two, then fill them up with the following: 4oz of cooked beef, 2-3 avocado slices, finely diced red onion, a handful of chopped cilantro, and your favorite sauce. You can use hot sauce, or my creamy sriracha sauce. Its in a FREE ebook on my substack if you want to check it out. Or you can use the recipe in step 4 below.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: 2 tbsp mayonnaise with 1⁄4 cup sriracha, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 1⁄2 tsp soy sauce, 2 tsp powdered monk fruit sweetener, the juice of 1 solid lime, 1 grated garlic clove, and 1 thinly sliced scallion or a handful of chopped cilantro. Season with salt and pepper, mix it up, taste and adjust as you wish.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Top with any other veggies you like, some chili flakes, sesame seeds, and ENJOY!

selected.title: Sweet potato filled with beef and sriracha sauce

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Stir together all sauce ingredients and adjust the salt, lime and heat.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Split the sweet potatoes and fill with the beef.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Top with avocado, red onion, cilantro and the creamy sriracha sauce.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Grab 4 skin-on bone-in chicken thighs, dab dry with a paper towel and add to a bowl.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: I first season the chicken. Drizzle with Chosen Foods Avocado Oil (I love this because its a neutral oil, made with 100% Pure Avocado Oil, and has a high smoke point), season with salt, pepper, and 1/2 tbsp each paprika, chili flakes, and onion powder. Mix well and let this ideally sit for 2 to 6 or longer (if longer, pop in the fridge).

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Preheat oven to 410F. Remove chicken from fridge and bring to room temp before putting in the oven.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: While that happens, make your sauce. I whisked together 4 tbsp Chosen Foods Classic Mayo, 2 tbsp red curry paste, the juice of 2 limes, & a small pinch salt. Whisk well, taste, adjust as you wish, and transfer 3/4 of it to a separate serving bowl. Brush remainder over the seasoned chicken thighs for even more flavor.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Place chicken on a wire rack and put in the oven at 410F for 35-40 mins. 6.Remove chicken from the oven, plate it. Again, you can serve this with whatever base you like, your salad of choice, and this sauce. . If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS. ENJOY! . . .

items.title: THAI CHICKEN DRUNKEN NOODLES (Pad Kee Mao)

expectedSnapshot.title: THAI CHICKEN DRUNKEN NOODLES (Pad Kee Mao)
ingredients: 430 g fresh rice noodles recommended, separated (or 200 g dried rice noodles, soaked as per packet instructions)
1 red bell pepper, sliced into matchsticks
1 bunch Chinese broccoli (about 3 cups), chopped - separate stems and leaves
2 Thai chilli’s deseeded and finely chopped
1 large handful of Thai basil leaves Sauce

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Marinate chicken and make noodle sauce: In a bowl, mix chicken with oyster sauce and light soy sauce. Set aside.In a small bowl, whisk together oyster, dark and light soysauces, coconut sugar, fish sauce, siracha and water.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Prepare noodles: If using fresh rice noodles, microwave for 3 minutes then carefully separate the noodles. If using dry rice noodles, soak for 4 minutes in boiling water. Do not oversoak as they will become mushy.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Cook chicken: Stir-fry chicken in 1 tbsp oil over medium high heat for 4-5 minutes until charred and cooked through. Remove.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Cook veg: Add 1 tbsp oil, then add garlic, shallots and chilli. Stir for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Add red pepper, carrot, and broccoli stems. Stir-fry for 3 mins, add broccoli leaves, cook for 2 more mins until leaves are wilted. Remove from pan.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Combine: Over very high heat, add noodles to the pan and fry for 30 seconds. Add sauce and gently toss for 2 mins being careful not to break the noodles. Add chicken and veggies back to the pan along with spring onions and Thai basil. Stir until glossy.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Serve: Plate hot and fresh with some chilli oil.

expectedSnapshot.title: Torta turca
ingredients: 1-2 small, chopped eggplants, lightly fried in oil.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: For the dough, combine the flour, salt, melted butter, and milk in a deep bowl. The dough should be soft and non-sticky. Transfer the dough to the counter and knead it by hand until smooth. Place the dough in a bag and chill in the refrigerator for 1 hour. Roll out the dough to the size of a serving plate. Place grated cheddar cheese in the center and top with the fried eggplant. Sprinkle with salt and red pepper flakes, then top with more cheddar cheese. Fold the dough as shown in the video, gently rolling out the dough without tearing it. Cook in a pan over medium heat until golden brown on both sides.

method.body: The FACTS behind MSG:👇

method.title: Step 4
method.body: MSG fear started in the 1960s from a letter to a medical journal, not a real study.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Media ran with it, food fear and racism piled on, and it stuck.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: When MSG was actually tested properly in blinded studies, the scary symptoms didn’t hold up.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: What the science shows 👇

method.title: Step 8
method.body: No consistent link to headaches

method.title: Step 9
method.body: No proven link to weight gain

method.title: Step 10
method.body: No evidence of brain damage

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Safe at normal intake levels

method.title: Step 12
method.body: Food safety authorities worldwide consider MSG safe.

method.title: Step 13
method.body: Some people feel off after very large doses, usually on an empty stomach. That’s individual tolerance, not toxicity, same as lactose or caffeine.

method.title: Step 14
method.body: The irony?

method.title: Step 15
method.body: People avoiding MSG still eat foods naturally high in glutamate every day, parmesan, tomatoes, mushrooms, slow cooked meats.

items.title: Viral Blanket Dumplings (High Protein + Meal Prep Friendly!)

expectedSnapshot.title: Viral Blanket Dumplings (High Protein + Meal Prep Friendly!)
ingredients: I freeze the extra dumpling filling into single serving portions so that whenever I want to make blanket dumplings I can have them fresh that day!

method.title: Step 1
method.body: In a large bowl, combine the lean beef mince with grated carrot, green onion, garlic, ginger, fresh chilli, soy sauce, fish sauce, rice wine vinegar, bone broth paste (if using), chicken stock paste, water, sweetener, and sesame oil.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Mix everything together really well until the filling becomes slightly sticky and well combined.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Let your dumpling wrappers sit at room temperature for 10–15 minutes while the filling rests.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Heat a non stick pan till hot, add oil.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Scoop 6-8 tbsps of the filling (ABOUT 1/4 of the total filling), let it brown for 30 seconds, then add each dumpling wrapper on top (like a blanket)

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Add 1/4 cup water & cover and let it cook for 8-10 minutes.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Serve immediately with chilli oil, soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, and extra spring onions.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Meal Prep Tip:

method.title: Step 9
method.body: I usually make a big batch of the mince filling and only use part of it fresh.

method.title: Step 10
method.body: The rest, I portion into single-serve freezer bags or containers and freeze.

method.title: Step 11
method.body: Then whenever I want blanket dumplings again, I just defrost a serve overnight in the fridge (or same day if needed), grab fresh dumpling wrappers, and make them fresh that day.

selected.title: Viral Blanket Dumplings (High Protein + Meal Prep Friendly!)
ingredients: ½ cup green onion, finely chopped
Fresh chilli, finely chopped, to taste

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Combine all filling ingredients and mix until the mixture becomes slightly sticky.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Let the filling rest while the dumpling wrappers come to room temperature for 10–15 minutes.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Heat a lightly oiled non-stick pan. Add 6–8 tablespoons of filling, approximately one quarter of the batch, and brown for 30 seconds.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Lay the dumpling wrappers over the filling like a blanket. Add ¼ cup water, cover and cook for 8–10 minutes.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Serve immediately with chilli oil, soy sauce, rice-wine vinegar and green onion. Freeze the unused filling in single-serving portions.

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Start with a Rotisserie chicken. Even though this is the Costco Rotisserie chicken, I went with a Whole Foods one for a better quality chicken. But use whatever rotisserie chicken you prefer. Remove from the bone, and dice up the chicken. Add it to the Rotisserie chicken bag.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Next, are the veggies. I finely diced 2 Persian cucumbers, 3 scallions (the white and green parts), and a large handful of cilantro. I like lots of cilantro. Add it on top of the chicken.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Add 1 to 2 tbsp of soy sauce or Tamari, 2 tbsp of garlic chili crunch, and lots of toasted sesame seeds.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Close the top of the Rotisserie bag, give it a good shake, then give it a taste and adjust with more sauce, sesame seeds, or anything else you like.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS! This was inspired by @tiffy.cooks but if you are the original creator let me know so I can share credit!

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Prep the shrimp. Grab 1 lb wild peeled, deveined shrimp. Butterfly down the middle and toss into a bowl with avocado oil, salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder, and a little Korean or Aleppo pepper flakes. Mix well.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Cook the shrimp. Grill if you can (they’re unreal on the grill), or use a cast iron pan. Medium-high heat, 90 seconds one side, 60–90 on the other. Don’t overcook or crowd the pan.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Make the sauce. Mix mayo, sesame oil, tomato paste, rice vinegar, soy sauce or tamari, lemon juice, smoked paprika, minced garlic, powdered monk fruit (or sweetener), and a pinch of salt. Taste and adjust.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Build the base. Boil an 8 oz bag of Kaizen rice in salted water for 6 minutes. (Or swap in regular rice/quinoa.)

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Assemble. Add rice to your bowl, top with shrimp, thin red onion, chopped cilantro, toasted sesame seeds, and a drizzle of yum yum sauce.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Quick, fresh, and protein-packed — perfect to prep a few bowls ahead for the week.

1 sheet puff pastry (about 9 ounces), thawed overnight in fridge
1 (6- to 7-ounce) block feta in brine, drained and finely crumbled
1 pound medium-thick asparagus, woody stems removed, trimmed to 81/2-inch spears
1 egg, beaten with 1 tablespoon of milk

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Step 1

method.title: Step 2

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Prepare the tart: Place the puff pastry on a sheet of parchment paper and roll it out so it measures approximately 10-by-13 inches. Using the parchment, carefully transfer the pastry to a baking sheet.

method.title: Step 7

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Using a small knife, lightly score a 3⁄4-inch border around the edge of the puff pastry. Scatter half the feta inside the scored border. Place the asparagus on top of the feta, lining them up and pressing them down so they sit flat. Brush about half the yuzu-miso glaze all over the asparagus. Season with salt and black pepper.

method.title: Step 9

method.body: Brush the border of the pastry with the egg-and-milk mixture. Scatter sesame seeds along the egg-washed edge. Drizzle 1 to 2 tablespoons of olive oil over the asparagus and the border, and place in the middle rack in the oven.

method.title: Step 11

Dot the remaining feta and the scallions on top and allow to cool for 5 minutes. (This allows the pastry to settle and makes it easier to cut.)

selected.title: Yuzu-Miso Glazed Asparagus Tart
selected.author: Ready In
ingredients: 2 tbsp yuzu juice, lemon juice or lime juice
1 block feta (6–7 oz), drained and finely crumbled
1 lb medium-thick asparagus, trimmed to 8½-inch spears

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Heat the oven to 220°C with a rack in the middle.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Whisk the miso, yuzu juice and honey to make the glaze.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Roll the puff pastry to about 10 by 13 inches and transfer it on parchment to a baking sheet.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Score a ¾-inch border. Scatter half the feta inside it, arrange the asparagus on top, brush with half the glaze and season.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Brush the border with the egg mixture, add sesame seeds and drizzle the tart with olive oil.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Bake for 18–20 minutes, until puffed and golden. Brush the asparagus with the remaining glaze, add the remaining feta and scallions, and cool for 5 minutes.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Cut into squares and serve hot or at room temperature.

items.title: Zingy Chicken Power Bowl: 476 cals, 54g protein

expectedSnapshot.title: Zingy Chicken Power Bowl: 476 cals, 54g protein

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Start with the chicken. I used about 2lb (900gr) skinless thighs (you can use your favorite cut). Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, then season with 1 tbsp each of onion powder, garlic powder, and paprika, 2 tsp ground pepper, and a generous couple pinches of salt.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Air fry, bake, or sear and finish in the oven at 400F for 37 to 40 minutes or until cooked through.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Make the salad: Slice 4 Persian cucumbers into thin half moons, quarter a big handful of cherry tomatoes, finely dice half a red onion, and chop a handful of fresh mint or lemon basil. Add the zest of a lemon, juice of a lemon, 2 tbsp olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. Toss well, taste and adjust.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: For the sauce: In a food processor, blend 1/2 cup Greek yogurt, 2 tbsp mayo, 1.5 tbsp olive oil, juice of 3 limes, 1 garlic clove, a big handful of basil and chives (2 to 3 tbsp each), salt and pepper. Add 1 to 2 tbsp cold water to thin if needed!

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Plate it up: Add rice, a few spoonfuls of salad, sliced or chopped chicken, and a few tablespoons of the basil-chive sauce. Finish with crumbled feta (about 1/4 oz per serving), chili flakes, & sesame seeds

method.title: Step 6
method.body: It doesn’t feel like a “healthy” meal... it just tastes really, really good

items.title: A EASY CHICKEN TIKKA SHEET PAN DINNER

expectedSnapshot.title: A EASY CHICKEN TIKKA SHEET PAN DINNER
ingredients: 1 red onion, cut into wedges 1 red bell pepper, sliced 1 green bell pepper, sliced

Mint-Cilantro Yogurt Sauce: 1 cup Greek yogurt 1/2 cup cilantro leaves 1/2 cup mint leaves 1-2 garlic cloves Juice of half large lemon 1 small green chili (optional) Pinch of salt 1–2 tbsp water to thin

method.title: Step 1
method.body: In a large bowl, combine all marinade ingredients and coat the chicken well. Marinate at least 2–6 hours (overnight is best).

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Cut the onion and peppers and set aside.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a sheet pan with parchment paper, spread out the chicken and veggies evenly, and drizzle lightly with olive oil. Roast for 30 minutes, then broil for 2-3 minutes to get that perfect char.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: While it roasts in the oven mix together all the yogurt sauce ingredients. Then taste and adjust for salt or lemon.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: When the chicken is done, remove, top with chili flakes, fresh chopped cilantro and mint, and strategically place dollops of the yogurt spread around it. You can serve this as is, with some rice, or if you are trying to eat high protein, my high protein kaizen rice.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS.

selected.title: A EASY CHICKEN TIKKA SHEET PAN DINNER
selected.author: ShredHappens
ingredients: 900 g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into chunks
½ tsp chilli powder or Aleppo pepper

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Combine the chicken with the yogurt, tomato paste, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic and spices. Cover and marinate for 2–6 hours, or overnight.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Arrange the chicken, onion and peppers on a parchment-lined sheet pan.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Roast for about 30 minutes, then broil or grill for 2–3 minutes until lightly charred and cooked through.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Blend or stir together the yogurt-sauce ingredients, adding enough water to make a spoonable sauce. Adjust the salt and lemon.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Serve the chicken and vegetables with the mint-cilantro yogurt sauce and, if desired, rice or quinoa.

items.title: PROTEIN STEAK WRAPS (AND FLATBREADS MADE WITH YOGURT!)

expectedSnapshot.title: PROTEIN STEAK WRAPS (AND FLATBREADS MADE WITH YOGURT!)
ingredients: 1 tsp olive oil 1/2 tbsp paprika 1/2 tbsp garlic powder 1/2 tbsp oregano Salt & pepper
Yogurt spread: 1 cup Greek yogurt 1 minced garlic clove 3–4 tbsp finely chopped dill Juice of half a lemon Pinch of salt & pepper
Onion salad: 1 small red onion thinly sliced 4 tbsp fresh parsley, finely chopped Juice of 1/2 lemon 1 tbsp sumac Salt

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Season steak - drizzle with olive oil, add the spices above, mix well, then airfry at 400F for 10 mins. Remove & let rest before slicing.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: For the flatbreads, mix flour, yogurt, baking powder, and salt until you have a nice dough. Divide into 4 equal pieces. Lay out parchment paper on a sheet pan, spray your hands & parchment paper with oil, and press each portion out into thin flatbreads. Create little spots with your fingers, and spray or brush lightly with oil, sprinkle with everything bagel, and pop in the oven at 375F for 18-20 mins (time varies by oven). Remove and let cool slightly, then cover with a towel so they stay soft.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Make the yogurt spread and the onion salad with the ingredients above, and grab some roasted red peppers and chop up some fresh parsley.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Then just assemble! Spread yogurt sauce, add roasted red pepper slices, 5oz steak, a large handful of the onion salad, and finish with extra parsley + sumac & enjoy.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: If you make it, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS

selected.title: PROTEIN STEAK WRAPS (AND FLATBREADS MADE WITH YOGURT!)
selected.author: ShredHappens
ingredients: ⅔ cup all-purpose flour or gluten-free all-purpose flour
Olive oil and everything-bagel seasoning, optional

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Coat the steak with olive oil and seasonings. Air-fry at 200°C for 10 minutes, then rest and slice.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Mix the flour, yogurt, baking powder and salt into a dough. Divide into four pieces and press into thin flatbreads on parchment. Brush lightly with oil, add seasoning if desired and bake at 190°C for 18–20 minutes.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Mix the yogurt-spread ingredients. Combine the onion-salad ingredients.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Spread the yogurt sauce over each flatbread and add roasted red pepper, sliced steak, onion salad, parsley and sumac.

expectedSnapshot.title: Puttanesca Poached Fish
ingredients: 1 large can crushed tomatoes, 28 ounces / approx. 800 g
1/2 cup dry white wine, approx. 120 ml
3/4 cup black or green olives, pitted and roughly chopped, approx. 100–110 g
2 tablespoons capers, rinsed well if salt-packed
4 skinless white fish fillets, about 6 ounces / 170 g each
Cod, halibut, hake, flounder, haddock or similar
Chopped fresh parsley, optional, for serving

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or high-sided sauté pan with a lid over medium-low heat.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Add the garlic, anchovies and crushed red pepper. Cook for 2–3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the anchovies dissolve and the garlic softens and begins to turn pale golden.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Add the crushed tomatoes, white wine, olives and capers. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer over medium-high heat.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Reduce the heat to medium-low and continue to simmer, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has slightly thickened, about 10 minutes.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Meanwhile, pat the fish fillets dry with paper towel and season them all over with black pepper and a little salt.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: Taste the sauce before adding more salt, as the anchovies, olives and capers are already salty. Adjust only if needed.

method.title: Step 7
method.body: Gently lay the fish fillets into the sauce. Cover and cook until the fish is opaque and just cooked through, 8–12 minutes, depending on the thickness of the fillets.

method.title: Step 8
method.body: Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if using.

method.title: Step 9
method.body: cua

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Finely dice 1 medium sweet potato. You’ll want this finely diced so it cooks faster. Drizzle with 1 tsp olive oil, season with salt, pepper, 2 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp chipotle powder, and 1/2 tsp sweet paprika. Add to a tray, pop in the oven for 20-25 mins at 400F.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Cook 1 lb lean ground beef (I used 93/7)) in 1 tsp olive oil. Season your beef with salt, pepper, 1 tbsp chipotle powder, 1 tbsp onion or garlic powder, 1 tbsp oregano, & 1 tbsp sweet paprika. Use a spoon or a spatula to mince it down to small pieces. I like to get the meat browned up really nicely, almost crispy, should take about 15-17 mins.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: When your sweet potato is roasted, divide it up in to 4 different serving bowls or meal prep containers. Add a large handful of finely shredded lettuce, 1/3 cup cottage cheese), 4oz of cooked beef, half a small avocado, finely diced red onion, a handful of chopped cilantro, and your favorite sauce. You can use hot sauce, or my creamy sriracha sauce. It’s in a FREE ebook on my substack if you want to check it out. Or you can use the recipe in step 4 below.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Top with any other veggies you like, some chili flakes, sesame seeds, and ENJOY!

method.title: Step 5
method.body: This is a great healthy lunch recipe, and perfect for meal prep.

method.title: Step 6
method.body: If you try it, let me know what you think on SHREDHAPPENS!

selected.title: TERNERA CON BATATA
ingredients: 1 lb lean ground beef, about 7% fat
⅓ cup cottage cheese per serving
1 scallion, thinly sliced, or chopped cilantro

method.title: Step 1
method.body: Toss the diced sweet potato with 1 tsp oil, salt, pepper, onion powder, chipotle powder and paprika. Roast at 200°C for 20–25 minutes.

method.title: Step 2
method.body: Cook the beef in the remaining oil. Season with salt, pepper, chipotle, onion or garlic powder, oregano and paprika, and brown well.

method.title: Step 3
method.body: Divide the roasted sweet potato among four bowls. Add lettuce, cottage cheese, beef, avocado, red onion and cilantro.

method.title: Step 4
method.body: Mix together the mayonnaise, sriracha, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sweetener, lime, garlic and scallion or cilantro.

method.title: Step 5
method.body: Spoon the sauce over the bowls and serve.
